author: Nick Smith
description: Academic planner built for teachers: build your timetable, plan lessons, track directed time, and keep notes, all inside your vault. Handles A/B weeks, cover, duties and calendar export. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Nick Smith
manifest.description: An academic planner built for teachers. Build your timetable, plan lessons, track directed time, and keep notes, all inside your vault.
manifest.name: Teacher Planner

name: Teacher Planner

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
*Released 28 June 2026*

![Teacher Planner 0.3.3, your planner now on your phone](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/main/docs/teacher-planner-0.3.3-feature.png)

This release is all about mobile. Teacher Planner was built for the desktop, and on a phone it showed. 0.3.3 rebuilds the planner, the timetable editor, the add event screen, and the date picker for a small touchscreen, so you can check your day, tick a lesson as prepared, or drop in a cover from your pocket. Everything you do on mobile still syncs with the same planner you use on your computer, because it is the same planner.

---

## What's new

### A planner made for your phone

On mobile the week grid is replaced by two views you switch between with tabs. **Day** shows the selected day as a clean list of cards, one per period, each with the class, room, and notes, and the same prepared tick and lesson plan link you have on the desktop, tap them straight from the card. **Week** lays the whole week out as a grouped list under day headers. A day strip across the top lets you jump between days, and the date and view controls sit on one tidy row with proper arrows, so nothing shifts around as you move through the week.

Tapping a lesson opens the same menu as the desktop, so prepared marks, plans, notes, and rescheduling are all there. Breaks and lunch show as slim rows, and a duty or meeting dropped into a free slot shows as a full card.

### Edit your timetable on mobile

The timetable editor is no longer a desktop grid squeezed onto a phone. On mobile it opens full screen as a day at a time list: pick a day, pick a week (A or B), and tap any period to assign, change, or clear a class. The class picker slides up from the bottom with search and the same custom start time, length, and room options as the desktop. Switch between your timetable templates with a dropdown, and create, rename, or delete them from a menu. On the desktop the drag and drop grid is unchanged.

### One calendar, everywhere

There is now a single date picker shared across the whole plugin, on both desktop and mobile. Tap the date in the planner, or the jump button in the Lessons overview, or the date in the add event screen, and the same clean month calendar opens. It always shows six full weeks with the surrounding days greyed in, so the buttons never jump around as you change month, today and your current selection are clearly marked, and dates outside your school year are greyed out. On the desktop it is now a compact, centred box rather than a large empty panel.

### Add event, rebuilt for mobile

The add event screen opens as a full screen sheet on a phone, with every field on its own row, large tap targets, and a Cancel and Add event bar pinned to the bottom. When the on screen keyboard opens, the sheet now shrinks to fit above it and scrolls, so the keyboard no longer hides the field you are typing into. The date field opens the shared calendar instead of the operating system's date popup.

---

## Smaller improvements

- **Lessons on mobile.** The Lessons overview now uses a dropdown to pick a class, giving the lesson list the full height of the screen instead of a wall of tiles.
- **Tidier controls.** Form fields across the mobile add event sheet and settings now share a single rounded rectangle shape, and the collapsible section headings in Settings keep their arrow on the same line as the title.
- **Clear of the system bars.** Lists, sheets, and buttons now leave room for Obsidian's floating navigation bar and your phone's status and navigation bars, so nothing hides behind them.

---

## Fixed

- **The keyboard no longer covers the add event form.** On Android the editing area now resizes around the keyboard so you can always see and scroll to the field you are filling in.
- **The calendar no longer jumps or leaves empty space.** Because it always draws six weeks, the month grid is a fixed height and the Today button stays put.
- **The week and day lists clear the bottom bar.** The last lesson of the day is no longer tucked behind Obsidian's floating toolbar.

---

## Updating

Update from Settings, then Community plugins, in Obsidian, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the release and drop them into `.obsidian/plugins/teacher-planner/`.

Teacher Planner requires Obsidian v1.7.2 or later. Your existing planners, timetables, and notes carry over automatically, so there is nothing you need to do.

---

If Teacher Planner saves you time, you can [buy me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/teacher.nsmith). It genuinely helps keep the project going. Found a bug or have an idea? [Open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/issues).

Happy planning!
releases.name: Teacher Planner 0.3.3: Your planner, now on your phone

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
*Released 25 June 2026*

Your weekly sidebar notes now format as you type, backed by a real Markdown file you can open anywhere. The planner finally blends with whatever Obsidian theme you use, light or dark, and lesson chips stay readable on every colour. There is a new zoom for the timetable editor, a confirmation step before anything is deleted, and a handful of fixes.

---

## ✨ What's new

### Week notes that format as you type

The week-notes panel in the sidebar is now a live editor. Bold, highlights, headings, checklists and links render as you type, instead of waiting for a preview. Each week's note is a real Markdown file in your vault, so it is searchable and linkable, and a button opens the full note in its own pane (new tab, a split, or the current pane, your choice in Settings). Your notes save as you go.

For new planners this is on by default. On an existing planner, turn on Settings, Notes, "Store week notes as vault files" and your current notes move across safely. If anything ever stops the rich editor loading, the panel quietly falls back to the plain text box, so you never lose access to your notes.

### A planner that blends with your Obsidian theme

The planner no longer ships its own colours. It now inherits your Obsidian theme's backgrounds, borders, text and accent, and follows light and dark automatically. Switch your Obsidian theme and the planner comes with you, no separate setting to keep in sync.

### Clearer chips on every theme

Lesson and event chips now pick readable text and icon colours based on the chip's own colour, so pale classes and bright colours stay legible. The "prepared" and "lesson plan" markers keep their meaning but sit cleanly on any chip, and the prepared tick switches between black and white so it never disappears into the colour behind it.

### Zoom the timetable editor

The timetable editor now has its own vertical zoom, both a control in the editor and a slider in Settings, so you can fit your whole day on screen or spread it out. The setting is per device, so each computer keeps its own comfortable size.

---

## 🔧 Smaller improvements

- **Confirm before deleting.** Removing an event or lesson, or deleting a subject, class, activity or block type, now asks first. You can switch the confirmations off in Settings, Reset if you prefer.
- **Clearer settings sections.** "Lessons" is now "My Classes", and the recurring-event sections read "Recurring Events: Directed Time" and "Recurring Events: Non-Directed Time".
- **Open week note where you like.** A new setting chooses whether the open-note button uses a new tab, a split, or the current pane.

---

## 🐞 Fixed

- **Editing an event opens cleanly.** The edit box no longer opens with the name field selected and the suggestion list popped over the form. The list only appears once you start typing in the title.
- **Keyboard menus open in the right place.** Opening a chip menu (or the week and overflow menus) with the keyboard now shows it next to what you selected, instead of in the corner of the screen.
- **The open-note button shows its icon**, and an empty week note now shows a gentle prompt instead of a blank space.

---

## ⬆️ Updating

Update from Settings, then Community plugins, in Obsidian, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the release and drop them into `.obsidian/plugins/teacher-planner/`.

Teacher Planner requires Obsidian v1.7.2 or later. Your existing planners, timetables and notes carry over automatically.

**One thing to check:** week notes saved as files are now named `Wn - <date>.md` (they used to be `Week note - <date>.md`). If you already kept week notes as files, rename them to the new `Wn - <date>.md` pattern in the same folder, otherwise those weeks will look empty. Notes kept inside the plugin data (not as files) are not affected.

---

The live note editor is built on the community EmbeddableMarkdownEditor by mgmeyers (the Obsidian Kanban plugin) and Fevol, used under the GPL-3.0 licence. **With MASSIVE thanks.**

If Teacher Planner saves you time, you can [buy me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/teacher.nsmith). It genuinely helps keep the project going. Found a bug or have an idea? [Open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/issues).

Happy planning! 📚
releases.name: Teacher Planner 0.3.2: Live week notes, and a planner that matches your theme

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
*Released 21 June 2026*

This release rebuilds the timetable editor and lets your day start on its own terms. The editor now gives every day its own blocks at their real times, so a Saturday or a sports afternoon finally fits, and a single popover handles everything about a slot. On top of that, lessons and one-off events can now begin partway through a period, the week grid is fully keyboard-friendly, and your class list and its lessons sit together. A long list of polish rounds it out.

---

## ✨ What's new

### A timetable editor rebuilt around your day

The editor is now a proper time-axis: a time gutter down the side and one column per day, with each day showing its own blocks at their real times. If a day runs a different shape, a Saturday, a sports afternoon, a half day, it appears in its own column with its own blocks instead of being squeezed into a shared row.

Editing a slot now happens in one place. Click a block and a single popover handles everything: the class, its start time, its length, and its room. Dragging still works exactly as before, drag a class to an empty cell to move it, onto another to swap the two, or hold Ctrl or Cmd to drop a copy. Short admin blocks like registration stay readable and expand on hover so nothing is ever cut off.

![Building a timetable](https://github.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/raw/0.3.1/docs/screenshots/timetable-editor.gif)

### Start lessons and events partway through a period

Not everything starts on the hour. A lesson or a one-off event can now begin midway through a period, a meeting at 09:40, say, or a lesson that picks up after registration. Set a start time when you add an event or edit a slot in the editor, and the week grid places it exactly right, with the free time before it shown clearly. Events that run across two periods are handled too, anchored to their real start.

### Move around with the keyboard

The week grid is now fully keyboard-friendly. Tab into it once, then use the arrow keys to move between cells and Enter or Space to open the one you're on. The focused cell is clearly outlined, including the empty cells, so you always know where you are.

### Your classes and their lessons, together

The Lessons overview no longer makes you flip between two screens. Your class list stays at the top and the lessons for whichever class you pick fill the space below, so you can move between classes and see their lessons at a glance.

---

## 🔧 Smaller improvements

- **A fresh colour for every new class.** Adding a class now picks a random colour that isn't already in use, instead of cycling through the palette in order, so a new class stands out straight away.
- **A and B week toggle, front and centre.** The "enable A and B week rotation" switch now sits at the very top of the timetable settings, and the setup wizard, where it belongs, since it shapes everything beneath it.
- **A room per slot.** You can now set a room directly on a slot in the editor, overriding the class's default room for that one placement.
- **Support the project from Settings.** A new section at the bottom of Settings lets you star the project on GitHub or buy me a coffee, alongside a link to the latest release notes.

---

## 🐞 Fixed

- **Empty cells show where you are.** When moving with the keyboard, an empty timetable cell now shows a clear focus outline instead of leaving you guessing.
- **No more gap behind a hovered lesson.** Hovering a lesson in the week view used to make its coloured band disappear and leave a gap; the block now stays put and simply grows to reveal the full details.
- **A clearer, steadier timetable editor.** A long round of editor polish: cleaner block labels and defined edges, text that adapts to the space it has, left-aligned rooms and hover details, and no more flicker when hovering short blocks.

---

## ⬆️ Updating

Update from Settings, then Community plugins, in Obsidian, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the release and drop them into `.obsidian/plugins/teacher-planner/`.

Teacher Planner requires Obsidian v1.7.2 or later. Your existing planners, timetables, and notes carry over automatically, so there is nothing you need to do.

---

If Teacher Planner saves you time, you can [buy me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/teacher.nsmith). It genuinely helps keep the project going. Found a bug or have an idea? [Open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/issues).

Happy planning! 📚
releases.name: Teacher Planner 0.3.1: A timetable editor that fits your week

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
*Released 16 June 2026*

This is the biggest release yet. The headline is the new **Lessons** overview: a single, year-long view of any class that you can edit in place and reschedule when life gets in the way. Alongside it, your directed time now counts the real length of each block, your school's shape can be saved as a reusable template, and the timetable editor finally lets you drag classes around. Backups have moved somewhere tidier too.

---

## ✨ What's new

### Plan a class across the whole year

The new **Lessons** button opens a dockable overview of a single class, from the first week of the year to the last. Pick a class and every one of its lessons is laid out in order, grouped into weeks with the A or B label in the header and centred on the current week, with a date search to jump anywhere. Holidays and INSET days are left out automatically, so what you see is your real teaching schedule.

Click any lesson to open it as a card and edit it right there. Type its note and room straight into the row, link or open a lesson plan, mark it prepared, attach a file or folder, or open its note, all without leaving the list. Anything you change here shows on the week grid for that lesson too, because it is the same lesson.

### Rescheduling that doesn't lose anything

When a lesson does not happen, you can shift the whole sequence along. Push your lessons forward a slot, pull them back, or insert a free lesson, and the planner moves everything for you. The note, room, plan link, and prepared mark all travel with the lesson, and your **markdown lesson notes are renamed to their new dates** so your vault stays organised (they are found by a small frontmatter block, so Obsidian keeps all your backlinks).

Anything that runs off the end of the year is parked safely in an Unplaced list rather than lost, and drops back in when you pull lessons back. Every shift has an undo.

### Save your school's setup as a template

Set your school up once and reuse it. You can now save two kinds of shareable template: a **School structure** template (your periods, block types, A and B pattern, school days, and year dates) and a **Holiday calendar** template (your holiday and INSET dates). Templates hold the shape of the school only, never your classes, timetable, notes, or hours.

Save and apply them from Settings, or start a brand-new planner from one in the setup wizard, with an optional date-shift when you load next year's holidays. You can also save a planner you have just built back out as a template, ready to hand to a colleague or use again next year.

### Drag classes around the timetable editor

Building or fixing a timetable is much quicker now. Drag a class chip to an empty cell to move it, drag it onto another class to swap the two, or hold Ctrl or Cmd while dragging to drop a copy. Drags respect the A or B week tab you are on and carry any custom duration with the class. Nothing is saved until you hit Save Timetable, so a mis-drag is undone by Cancel.

### Directed time that follows the block

Your directed-time total now counts the real length of the block each item sits in, instead of a fixed default. Registration counts its few minutes, a full period counts the hour, and so on. A per-placement override still wins whenever something runs short or long, just click the duration badge on a block in the timetable editor, and an overridden block is clearly tinted and marked. On the grid, an item shorter than its block now sizes to its real length, with the free time shown beneath and the exact range on hover.

---

## 🔧 Smaller improvements

- **Backups out of the way.** Backups and templates now live inside the plugin's own folder, so they no longer clutter your file explorer and survive plugin updates. The export tool lets you tick which planners to save and choose where they land, the plugin's library, a vault folder, or anywhere on your computer, and you can import a backup from the library or any file.
- **Tidier settings.** Collapsible section headers now use a crisp chevron with a separator line beneath, and a couple of headings read more cleanly.
- **Bulk plan apply removed.** "Apply plan to future lessons" is gone from the cell menu; the new Lessons overview is the place to manage a class across the year.

---

## 🐞 Fixed

- **The A and B week toggle refreshes straight away.** Turning rotation on or off, or changing the start week, now updates the week view immediately instead of staying stale until you reloaded the plugin.
- **One note and room per lesson, everywhere.** Notes and room are now a single value shared between the overview and the week grid, with a per-lesson change overriding the recurring default. Room pre-fills from the block's actual room rather than leaving most lessons blank.
- **Pulling lessons back never deletes one.** A slot that looked blank but held a prepared mark, plan link, or attachment is now parked safely instead of being overwritten.
- **Holidays and INSET days stay out of the overview**, matching the week grid and the directed-time tracker, so counts and shift positions stay correct across breaks.

---

## ⬆️ Updating

Update from Settings, then Community plugins, in Obsidian, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the release and drop them into `.obsidian/plugins/teacher-planner/`.

Teacher Planner requires Obsidian v1.7.2 or later. Your existing planners, timetables, and notes carry over automatically, so there is nothing you need to do.

---

If Teacher Planner saves you time, you can [buy me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/teacher.nsmith). It genuinely helps keep the project going. Found a bug or have an idea? [Open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/issues).

Happy planning! 📚
releases.name: Teacher Planner 0.3.0: Plan a whole class, a whole year

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
*Released 15 June 2026*

This release is all about the messy reality of a school week. The Add Event tool has grown into a proper one-off event creator, the planner now warns you when two things land in the same slot, and the whole thing finally feels at home on a phone. There is plenty more under the hood too, from per-device zoom to drag-to-copy.

---

## ✨ What's new

### A proper one-off event creator

Real weeks are full of things that are not on your timetable. A meeting, a cover lesson, a trip, a duty, a parents' evening. The Add Event tool has been rebuilt from the ground up to handle all of them.

Give an event any name you like, or start typing and search your existing classes and activities to fill it in. Pick a single block or several at once, set a colour from the palette or a custom picker, choose whether it counts as directed time, and set a duration that fills itself in from the blocks you chose. Every new event even opens with a random colour, so a busy week stays easy to read.

When an event covers two or more empty blocks next to each other, those blocks join into one clean spanning block. If a lesson is in the way, the event simply shows as a normal chip in each block instead, so a lesson is never hidden.

### A warning when you double-book yourself

Put two things in the same slot and the planner now spots it. A small marker appears on the block, and it turns red when the directed-time tracker is on and the overlap would count that time twice.

Add an event onto a slot that is already in use and you get a clear prompt rather than a silent clash. You can keep both, add the event without counting it as directed time, or remove what was already there. Your directed-time total is never quietly changed, you are simply told and given the choice.

### Built for your phone

On a phone the week view now offers three layouts. Day gives you one full-width, readable day with a tap-to-switch day picker. Agenda lists the whole week as a simple scroll. Grid keeps the familiar timetable. The planner remembers which you prefer, and the desktop view is exactly as it was.

### Smarter A and B weeks

If your school runs a two-week timetable, the rotation now counts teaching weeks and skips full holiday weeks, so it stays in step right across a half term instead of drifting. The week badge is now a button, so a single click sets a one-off A or B swap for just this week, or from this week onward, for those terms that start on the opposite week.

### Backups you can rely on

You can now export any planner, or all of them at once, to a backup file in your vault, and import a backup back in as a new planner. Best of all, deleting a planner now saves a backup first, so a delete is always recoverable.

---

## 🔧 Smaller improvements

- **Jump to any week.** The week navigation now has clear previous and next arrows and a centre button that opens a date picker, so you can land on any week, or jump back to today, in one tap.
- **Per-device grid zoom.** A new setting sets the height of the week grid. It is kept per device, so your laptop and your phone each remember their own zoom and it never syncs between them.
- **Drag to copy.** Hold Ctrl or Cmd while dragging a lesson or event to drop a copy instead of moving it.
- **Prepared tick on events.** The green prepared marker now works on one-off events as well as timetabled lessons.
- **Tidier timetable picker.** Class rows now show the subject emoji, and opening the picker on a filled slot highlights the item already there.

---

## 🐞 Fixed

- **Dragging a one-off event now changes its period.** Previously a single-period event dragged to a new period only changed day. It now lands in the period you drop it on, while multi-period events still move by day and keep their span.

---

## ⬆️ Updating

Update from Settings, then Community plugins, in Obsidian, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the release and drop them into `.obsidian/plugins/teacher-planner/`.

Teacher Planner requires Obsidian v1.7.2 or later. Your existing planners, timetables, and notes carry over automatically, so there is nothing you need to do.

---

If Teacher Planner saves you time, you can [buy me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/teacher.nsmith). It genuinely helps keep the project going. Found a bug or have an idea? [Open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/issues).

Happy planning! 📚
releases.name: Teacher Planner 0.2.9: Plan one-off weeks without the headache

Happy planning! 📚
releases.name: # Teacher Planner 0.2.8: Plan one-off weeks without the headache

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
# Teacher Planner 0.2.6 — Plan smarter, see more at a glance

*Released 13 June 2026*

This release is all about **clarity and control** — knowing what's ready for the week at a single glance, naming your notes the way you want, and keeping your weekly notes as proper, searchable files in your vault. Plus a taller, easier-to-read week grid and an important reliability fix.

---

## ✨ What's new

### Name your notes, your way
Lesson and event notes now get their filenames from a template **you** control, set in **Settings → Note titles** with a live preview as you type. Mix and match tokens for the date, period, class, subject — and even the subject's emoji.

- Lessons default to: `13-06-2026 - P1 - 10A - 🌱 Biology`
- Events default to: `13-06-2026 - Break - Bake sale`

Empty fields tidy themselves up automatically, so a missing value never leaves an awkward gap, and anything that can't go in a filename is cleaned out for you.

### See what's ready — right on the lesson
Each lesson chip can now show small status icons in the corner, so a glance across your week tells you what's prepared and what isn't:

- **🟢 Plan linked** — a green document icon when a lesson plan note is attached. Click it to open the plan.
- **✅ Prepared** — click the tick to mark a lesson "prepared". Perfect if you don't use plan notes but still want an at-a-glance "this one's sorted" marker. It's saved per lesson and follows the lesson if you move it.
- **📎 Attachments** — link an external file (paperclip) or folder (folder icon) from anywhere on your computer, and open it straight from the chip.

![Linking a lesson plan](linking-lesson-plan.gif)

![Attaching a folder and marking a lesson prepared](linking-folder-marking-prepared.gif)

### Weekly notes as real vault files (optional)
Your sidebar week notes can now be saved as proper markdown files — one per week, named `Week note - <Monday date>` — so they're **searchable, linkable, and backlink-friendly**, and they no longer bloat the plugin's data file. It's opt-in: turn it on in **Settings → Notes** and your existing week notes are moved across for you, into their own tidy folder.

---

## 🔧 Smaller improvements

- **A roomier week grid** — periods are now a little taller (the time axis went from 108 to 120 pixels per hour), so busy days are easier to read.
- **Cleaner lesson chips** — the plan indicator is now a crisp green document icon instead of a boxed dot, and the faint "no plan" ring is gone, so the corner only shows what's actually there. The classroom and status icons share one neat line that reflows gracefully on small screens and mobile.
- **Tidier settings** — note-storage options now live in their own **Notes** section.

---

## 🐞 Fixed

- **Week-note dates and a file-mode edge case.** Week notes were keyed using UTC, which meant that during British Summer Time a Monday could be saved under the *Sunday* date — and, in one specific case, enabling "store week notes as files" could blank that week's note. Week notes now always use the correct Monday date, existing notes are migrated automatically, and the switch-to-files process is now fully safe: it never overwrites real content, verifies every file before tidying up, and won't replace a saved note with an empty one.

---

## ⬆️ Updating

Update from **Settings → Community Plugins** in Obsidian, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the release and drop them into `.obsidian/plugins/teacher-planner/`.

**Requires Obsidian v1.7.2 or later.** Your existing planners, timetables, and notes carry over automatically — there's nothing you need to do.

---

If Teacher Planner saves you time, you can [buy me a coffee](https://buymeacoffee.com/teacher.nsmith) — it genuinely helps keep the project going. Found a bug or have an idea? [Open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/NSDerred/teacher-planner-obsidian/issues).

Happy planning! 📚
releases.name: Teacher Planner 0.2.6

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
- Settings sections wouldn't expand: a leftover .tp-collapsible-content { display:none } rule from the 0.2.2 collapsible rework overrode the class-based toggle (no console error — JS worked, CSS never showed content).

- Current-time line now spans the whole week as one continuous line from the time gutter, drawn above all blocks; previously it rendered only inside today's column, appearing hidden behind blocks.
releases.name: 0.2.5: fix settings sections not expanding, continuous current-time line

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
- xlsx → write-excel-file (write-only): dependency advisory GHSA-4r6h-8v6p-xvw6 no longer applies, bundle shrinks ~420 KB; exports produce identical sheets/data via new buildXlsx helper, validated headlessly.

- Fix remaining source warnings: unnecessary PeriodType assertion (AddPeriodModal), typed JSON.parse in settings revert (SettingsTab).

- README Security section updated for the new dependency.
releases.name: 0.2.4: replace xlsx with write-excel-file, clear final bot warnings

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
- Fixes event chips throwing "getEventPlan is not defined" (missing import) on click — events can be clicked and edited again.

- Adaptive hover: the hover card now fits its content exactly — no more blank space below short chips; block footprint kept as the hover hit-area so nothing flickers.

- svelte-check + npm run verify pipeline: .svelte files are now fully type-checked (how the missing import escaped); all 40 latent type errors fixed.

- Last three window.confirm() → ConfirmModal (bulk apply, undo, delete template); fixed "Save Timetable" passing the MouseEvent as the force flag, bypassing the past-week warning.

- Bot-audit zero pass: no innerHTML, console.log, or any-casts remain; carries the earlier zero-warning work (typing, deprecations, a11y, release CI).
releases.name: 0.2.3: fix unclickable event chips, adaptive hover cards, svelte-check gate

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
Feature release. Highlights:

- Calendar (.ics) export: new iCal format in the export modal alongside
  Excel/CSV — import your timetable into Google/Apple/Outlook calendar.
  Content toggles (lessons, date events, holidays & INSET as all-day,
  breaks & registration), Mon–Sun day selector, and a from/to date range
  defaulting to today → year end. Dependency-free RFC 5545 generator that
  resolves A/B rotation, template changes, holidays, slot exclusions and
  moved lessons exactly as the week view does.

- Day schedules: days can have different period structures (sports
  afternoons, half-day Saturdays). New DaySchedule model with automatic
  migration; manage schedules and assign days in Settings → School
  Timetable or directly in wizard Step 7. iCal export and directed time
  resolve periods per day.

- Time-axis week view: complete redesign — time gutter with hour lines,
  each day column independently shaped by its schedule, period blocks
  positioned by real times with lesson/event chips inside, gapped column
  layout with hairline block borders, column-level holiday/INSET shading,
  now-line on the axis. All interactions preserved (drag-drop with
  invalid-drop rejection, chip menus, lesson notes, A/B badges).

- Setup wizard: day-schedule editing in Step 7 (create/rename/delete,
  day pills, per-schedule periods); rename/new dialogs use a proper modal
  (window.prompt is disabled in Obsidian).

- Fixes: delete-planner save race; real calendar-date validation; period
  start<end validation; overlapping holiday/INSET detection; emoji picker
  listener leak; drag-reject flash; period name no longer crushed by the
  time label on narrow panes.

- Docs: README rewritten with new screenshots and full-length GIF demos;
  CHANGELOG 0.2.0 section.

Full details in CHANGELOG.md.
releases.name: 0.2.0 — iCal export, day schedules, time-axis week view

releases.author: NSDerred
releases.description:
Settings — School Timetable
- Sort periods by start time on every render so the editor always
  displays them in chronological order, regardless of how they were
  entered or imported. Reordering persists on save.

Setup Wizard — School periods (Step 7)
- Add a block-type dropdown to each period row, populated from the
  block types defined earlier in the wizard. New periods default to
  the first defined block type instead of a hardcoded "lesson".

Setup Wizard — Clear all
- Add a confirm-guarded "Clear all" button to both the Holidays & INSET
  step (Step 4) and the School periods step (Step 7).

Setup Wizard — default holiday labels
- Rename default holidays: "Christmas" → "Winter Break",
  "Easter" → "Spring Break".

Settings — Edit active planner (new)
- Add an "Edit" button to the active planner card opening a new
  EditPlannerModal (src/modals/EditPlannerModal.ts) to change the
  planner name and academic-year start/end dates after creation.
- On save, timetable templates shift by the same day-delta as the
  start date so the timetable and A/B week rotation realign to the
  corrected range. Independent dated content (holidays, INSET, date
  events, slot exclusions, week notes, lesson-note files) is left
  untouched. Validates date format and ordering; active planner only.

Also repaired truncated tails in SettingsTab.ts and SetupWizardModal.ts
that were preventing the build.
releases.name: Editable planner dates, wizard improvements, timetable sort fix

releases.description: README placeholder fixes and security note. See CHANGELOG.md for details.
